mechanism_narrative: Sustained real GDP growth signals strong aggregate demand, which increases corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward 60-day returns is below 0.03, or the proportion of positive returns following GDP growth >2% is not significantly above 65%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer income and spending, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: More than 40% of triggered instances produce positive 20-day forward returns, or the mean return difference between triggered and non-triggered periods is not negative at p<0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When both the federal funds rate and headline inflation are elevated, it indicates that monetary tightening is actively compressing real earnings and increasing discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over weekly periods.
failure_condition: The interaction term FEDFUNDS * CPIAUCSL in a linear regression predicting 5-day forward returns has a non-significant coefficient (p>0.10) or the partial correlation of the interaction with returns is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is above its 12-month moving average, it signals a supply expansion phase that underpins earnings growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; the signal flips to negative below that threshold.
failure_condition: In periods when INDPRO is below its 12-month MA, the mean 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05), or the difference in returns between above-MA and below-MA regimes is not significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent consumer price inflation erodes the real value of future equity cash flows and prompts tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC between CPIAUCSL year-over-year change and 20-day forward returns stays above 0.04, or the proportion of negative returns following inflation above 3% is not significantly greater than 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real retail sales greater than 2% signals an abrupt drop in consumer spending, which leads to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: More than 30% of triggered instances produce positive 60-day returns, or the average return in triggered windows is not lower than the unconditional average with p<0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls are growing yet the unemployment rate remains very low, it signals an overheated labor market that forces the Fed to tighten, compressing valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term PAYEMS_growth * UNRATE in a regression predicting 5-day returns is not significant at p<0.10, or the mean return in tight labor market periods with PAYEMS growth >200k is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal robust construction activity, strong consumer confidence, and positive economic momentum, which lifts corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Rank IC between HOUST growth and 5-day forward returns is below 0.03, or the proportion of positive returns following HOUST > 1.5 million is not above 70%.
